expectations.text: Output is exactly one self-contained .html file — no separate CSS/JS/asset files are required to open it
expectations.evidence: Exactly one .html file (index.html, 918 lines). All CSS is in an inline <style> block (lines 7-284) and all JS in an inline <script> block (lines 368-916). grep for '<link', 'src=', '@import', 'url(' returned zero matches — no external references of any kind, so it opens standalone. Extra non-required artifacts present (allowed per rule, listed): README.md, screenshot-1-intake-queue.png, screenshot-2-dispatch-board.png, screenshot-3-assign-picker.png, screenshot-4-closeout.png, screenshot-5-end-of-day.png.

expectations.text: No network data calls: the file contains no fetch()/XMLHttpRequest/axios usage (CDN <script>/<link> tags are allowed)
expectations.evidence: grep -niE 'fetch\(|XMLHttpRequest|axios|<link|src=' over index.html returned zero matches. The file has no network access at all (not even CDN tags); the only timers are tickClock (line 914) and the toast auto-hide setTimeout (line 548).

expectations.text: Demo data is hardcoded in the file, and interactions mutate it in memory so the UI updates without a page reload
expectations.evidence: seed() (lines 384-533) hardcodes 14 techs and 17 jobs into module-level arrays `techs`/`jobs`. Mutations operate on these arrays in memory: assignJob (lines 565-576) sets job.assigned_tech/status, advanceJob (578-591) walks assigned→enroute→onsite→done, closeOutApprove (593-603) writes parts/time/flag and sets status 'closed', closeOutBounce (605-612), dropJob (740-756). Every mutation ends with renderAll() (lines 897-902) which re-renders queue, board, EOD and chrome via innerHTML — no reload, no persistence. resetDemo() re-seeds (line 614).

expectations.text: All four screens from the spec exist: intake queue, dispatch board (techs as columns with job cards), job detail, and end-of-day summary
expectations.evidence: (1) Intake queue: section#screen-queue (line 310) rendered by renderQueue() with sort dropdown. (2) Dispatch board: section#screen-board (line 327); renderBoard() (694-724) emits one .col div per tech (techs.map, line 708) plus an Unassigned column, each containing job cards from jobCardHTML(). (3) Job detail: #detailOverlay (line 357) + openDetail() (795-856) — full-job overlay with all fields, status-history timeline, and close-out form (implemented as a modal sheet rather than a nav tab, but it is a complete job-detail view per the spec's content: 'everything about one job, status history, close-out form'). (4) End-of-day: section#screen-eod (line 339) + renderEod() (860-894) with closed/emergencies/follow-ups/open stats, per-tech recap table, closed-jobs table.

expectations.text: A job can be assigned from the intake queue to a tech and then appears on that tech's board column
expectations.evidence: Traced click path: queue card 'Assign →' button (line 669) → openPicker(jobId) (760) → clicking a non-off pick-row fires assignJob(jobId, techName) (774) → assignJob sets job.assigned_tech = techName and job.status = 'assigned' (570-571), then renderAll(). renderBoard() builds each tech column from techJobs(name) = jobs.filter(j => j.assigned_tech === name && j.status !== 'closed') (line 542), so the newly assigned job renders as a card in that tech's column (lines 708-720). Drag-and-drop assignment also exists (dragStart line 728, dropJob line 740 → assignJob).

expectations.text: Emergency-priority jobs are visually loud (distinct alert/red styling), per the spec's 'unassigned + emergency needs to be LOUD' note
expectations.evidence: Multiple red/alert treatments: .queue-card.is-emergency gets a 2px solid red border, red-tinted background, and a pulsing cardThrob box-shadow animation (lines 133-140); .chip.emergency is solid red with white text (line 94); .job-card.is-emergency gets a 5px red left border + red-tinted bg (line 190); the nav queue badge gets class 'alarm' with a pulsing red animation when unassigned emergencies exist (lines 58-62, 622); a full-width pulsing red #emergencyBanner ('N EMERGENCIES UNASSIGNED — oldest waiting...') shows on every non-queue screen (lines 72-79, 624-630); and the board's Unassigned column gets .has-emergency (red border + throb, line 168). Two unassigned emergencies are seeded (Harold Brink, Priya Raman) so this is visible on load.

expectations.text: The close-out flow captures the spec's fields: parts used, time on site, and a needs-follow-up flag
expectations.evidence: When a job has status 'done', openDetail renders the close-out form (lines 821-832): textarea#coParts ('Parts used'), input#coTime type=number ('Time on site (min)'), and checkbox#coFollow ('Needs follow-up visit'). closeOutApprove (593-603) reads all three into job.parts_used, job.time_on_site, job.needs_followup before setting status 'closed'. A 'Bounce back to tech' path also exists (closeOutBounce, 605-612), matching the spec's 'reviews and closes, or bounces back'.

expectations.text: The hardcoded data uses the spec's job fields (zone, equipment, priority, status, assigned tech)
expectations.evidence: Every seeded job literal includes zone, equipment, priority, status, assigned_tech — e.g. lines 417-419: zone: 'N', equipment: 'Furnace', priority: 'emergency', status: 'unassigned', assigned_tech: null. The J() factory (405-413) also adds the rest of the spec's field list: id, phone, time_on_site, parts_used, needs_followup, plus customer, address, issue, created_at and a history array. Verified 14 techs and 5 unassigned seed jobs via node.

expectations.text: A too-long customer name (like the spec's 'Greater Metropolitan Property Management LLC, Building C') is included so overflow handling is visible
expectations.evidence: Line 420 seeds the exact name 'Greater Metropolitan Property Management LLC, Building C' with an equally long address ('8800 Industrial Pkwy W, Suite 240-260, rear loading entrance'). Overflow handling exists where it renders: .queue-card .cust and .addr use overflow:hidden + text-overflow:ellipsis + white-space:nowrap (lines 142-146), board cards' .jc-cust likewise (line 191), the EOD customer cell has max-width + ellipsis (line 887), and full text is recoverable via title attributes (e.g. lines 659, 686) and word-break in the detail sheet (line 214).

expectations.text: An empty intake queue state is reachable and does not look broken, per the spec's 'slow afternoons' note
expectations.evidence: Reachable by clicking: 5 unassigned jobs are seeded; clicking 'Assign →' on each card and picking any available tech in the picker removes it from unassigned() (status becomes 'assigned'). When unassigned() is empty, renderQueue renders a designed empty state (lines 646-650): a dashed-border panel with a checkmark, 'Queue is clear', and the hint 'New requests from calls and the web form will appear here. Nothing is waiting.' The board's Unassigned column similarly shows 'Nothing waiting ✓' (line 705) instead of collapsing. The README's demo script step 7 explicitly walks this path.

expectations.text: A loading state and an error message/toast are each reachable by clicking
expectations.evidence: No loading state exists anywhere in the file: grep for 'loading|spinner|skeleton' matches only the address string 'rear loading entrance' (line 420), and the only setTimeout is the toast auto-hide (line 548) — there is no simulated-delay, spinner, or skeleton UI in any flow. The error half is also weak: the only error-style message is the 'X is off duty — pick someone else.' toast in assignJob (line 568), but in the click-based picker, off-duty techs render as disabled rows with NO onclick handler (lines 773-774), so clicking them does nothing; the error toast is reachable only via drag-and-drop onto an off-duty column (dropJob line 755 → assignJob), which is not a click path. All other toasts (assignment, close, bounce, reset) are success confirmations, not errors. Both required states fail the 'reachable by clicking' bar.

claims.claim: README: 'all 14 techs' appear on the dispatch board
claims.evidence: seed() defines exactly 14 techs (lines 387-402, counted via node); renderBoard maps over all of them.

claims.claim: README: 'no server, no internet, no build step' — double-click index.html to run
claims.evidence: Zero external references or network calls found by grep; everything is inline.

claims.claim: README: pulsing red queue badge + sitewide banner when an emergency is unassigned
claims.evidence: renderChrome (617-634) toggles badge class 'alarm' and shows #emergencyBanner when emergencies exist and currentScreen !== 'queue'.

claims.claim: README: 'Off-duty techs are locked out' of assignment
claims.evidence: Picker rows for off techs have no onclick (lines 773-774); assignJob guards tech.status === 'off' (line 568); dragOver shows drop-blocked styling (lines 733-739).

claims.claim: README: the close-out review 'opens automatically' when a job is marked done
claims.evidence: advanceJob onsite→done branch calls renderAll(); openDetail(jobId) (line 588).

claims.claim: README: emergencies seeded with 'waiting 52 min' warning visible on load
claims.evidence: Harold Brink emergency created_at = now - 52*min (line 419); age >= 45 min gets red 'old' class and a warning glyph (lines 654, 666).

user_notes_summary.note: No user_notes.md, metrics.json, or transcript was present in the outputs directory; grading is based on direct code inspection of index.html plus README.md.

suggestions.assertion: A loading state and an error message/toast are each reachable by clicking
suggestions.reason: This bundles two independent outcomes into one pass/fail bit. This run had a (drag-reachable) error toast but no loading state at all — splitting into two assertions would show which half the skill actually fixes, which is the discriminating signal this eval seems designed to capture (the source spec never mentions loading/error states, so this is pure skill-driven behavior).

No assertion covers the EOD summary updating live from close-outs (stats are recomputed from the jobs array in renderEod, lines 860-867). A prototype with static EOD numbers would pass every current assertion; checking 'closing a job increments the EOD counts' would catch that.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions are concrete and code-verifiable; 10 of 11 are genuinely discriminating. Only flag: the combined loading+error assertion hides which half failed.
